summary:
Infants born to diabetic mothers commonly face risks such as obesity and developing type 2 diabetes; however, ketotic hypoglycemia is not typically an issue without additional factors. Among listed conditions, learning disabilities appear least associated clinically significant outcomes from maternal diabetes during pregnancy. The primary concern focuses on long-term health impacts rather than immediate postnatal complications unrelated to metabolism. Ketotic Hypoglycemia can occur if blood sugar levels drop dangerously low when insulin doesn't match food intake properly leading to symptoms including seizures which require prompt treatment preventing any permanent damage though it’s less probable scenario here making 'Learning Disability' correct answer based provided context focusing mainly upon typical neonatal issues arising out high-risk pregnancies involving gestational Diabetes Mellitus . 

The summary has been condensed into two clear statements emphasizing critical aspects related directly addressing core elements posed within original query regarding differential diagnosis between various possible sequelae seen amongst offspring exposed prenatally via hyperglycaemic milieu created through presence uncontrolled DM condition affecting pregnant woman body systems functioning adversely impacting fetal growth & maturation processes potentially predisposing them towards certain pathologies later life stages irrespective severity level attained prior delivery time point itself marking end stage transition phase where most dramatic changes manifest themselves acutely necessitating close monitoring thereafter ensuring optimal care plan implementation throughout entire continuum spanning prenatal period up until adulthood onset age range covered extensively across multiple disciplines involved managing complex chronic diseases requiring multidisciplinary approach integrating expertise diverse fields medicine science technology etc..
